steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends with "Therefore, it isn't surprising that ______" — a conclusion drawn from the evidence. The key evidence: bulbuls disperse more seeds from non-native plants (like night-blooming jessamine) than from at-risk native plants (like the lapalapa tree), because non-natives are far more abundant. The blank must be a logical consequence of that seed-dispersal imbalance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "On Oahu, the rate of population increase is higher for non-native plants than it is for vulnerable native forest plants, like lapalapa trees.": more seeds dispersed from non-native plants $\Rightarrow$ faster population growth for those plants. This follows directly from the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — "Night-blooming jessamine shrubs and other non-native forest plants only recently began to outnumber native forest plants in the wild.": timing of when non-natives started to outnumber natives is not discussed in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — "On Oahu, native plants, such as the lapalapa tree, are already growing in most of the spots where red-whiskered bulbuls would be likely to drop plant seeds.": where natives are currently growing is not addressed in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — "The lapalapa tree and the night-blooming jessamine shrub are the plant species most in danger of going extinct in the wild.": the passage says lapalapa is at risk of extinction; it says nothing of the sort about jessamine, which is thriving. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
